title: DAML Bug Benchmark Case
description: Unified schema for DAML security/correctness benchmark cases derived from audits, release notes, documentation patterns, and public codebases.

id.description: Unique identifier. Convention: SOURCE-ID (e.g. TEMPLE-HAL-03, CANTON-REL-2.9.0-01, DOC-AUTH-01).

title.description: Short descriptive title of the finding or pattern.

artifact_type.description: What kind of benchmark artifact this represents.

source_title.description: Human-readable name of the source document or report.

source_url.description: Public URL of the source, or null if the source is private/local.

date.description: Date of the finding, report, or release (ISO 8601).

target_object.description: The primary DAML template, choice, or function affected (e.g. 'TokenAdmin.MintTokens').

affected_modules.description: List of DAML module files affected (e.g. ['Temple/Transfer.daml', 'Temple/Registry.daml']).

pattern_family.description: Top-level bug pattern family. auth=authorization/signatory; visibility=observer/disclosure; temporal=time/deadline; lifecycle=create/archive/state-machine; invariant=data-consistency/validation; scalability=growth/DoS; upgrade=package/version; integration=API/spec-conformance; diagnostics=error-messages/audit-trail/dead-code.

pattern_name.description: Specific pattern name within the family (e.g. 'missing_observer', 'phase_separation_violation', 'unilateral_archive').

severity.description: Severity as assessed by the source (or inferred for non-audit sources).

bvss_score.description: BVSS score from the audit report, if available.

evidence_strength.description: How confident we are that this represents a real bug/pattern. strong=auditor-confirmed finding with code; moderate=release-note or architectural review; weak=inferred from docs/changelog; synthetic=manually constructed example.

explicit_bugfix.description: Whether a concrete code fix exists and is referenced.

explicit_security.description: Whether the source explicitly labels this as a security issue.

inferred_pattern.description: Whether the pattern classification was inferred by us (true) or stated by the source (false).

synthetic.description: Whether the DAML code samples were synthetically constructed rather than extracted from real code.

before_version.description: Git commit hash or version tag of the vulnerable/pre-fix state.

after_version.description: Git commit hash or version tag of the fixed/post-fix state.

before_after_available.description: Whether we have both buggy and fixed code samples for this case.

description.description: Detailed technical description of the bug or pattern.

recommendation.description: Recommended fix or mitigation as stated by the source.

buggy.description: Relative path to the buggy DAML code snippet file, or null.

fixed.description: Relative path to the fixed DAML code snippet file, or null.

diff.description: Relative path to the diff/patch file, or null.

code_snippets.description: Paths to code sample files relative to the case directory.

type.description: Classification of the oracle check that would detect this bug.

description.description: Human-readable description of what a correct oracle/checker would verify.

static_signal.description: What a static analysis tool could look for (e.g. 'choice controller not in observers', 'no ensure clause checking field > 0').

symbolic_property.description: Property that symbolic execution could verify (e.g. 'forall t: execute(t) => t >= allocateBefore').

oracle.description: Oracle information for automated detection tool development.

good_for_static.description: Whether this case is a good candidate for static analysis tool evaluation.

good_for_symbolic.description: Whether this case is a good candidate for symbolic execution tool evaluation.

good_for_benchmark.description: Whether this case should be included in the primary benchmark set.
